94	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
95	
96	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
97	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
98	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
99	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
100	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
101	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
102	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
103	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
104	
105	namespace PresheafOfModules
106	
107	universe v'
108	
109	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
110	
111	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
112	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
113	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
114	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
115	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
116	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
117	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
118	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
119	  naturality {X Y} f := by
120	    ext r
121	    dsimp
122	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
123	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
124	    simp only [map_one]
125	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
126	
127	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
128	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
129	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
130	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
131	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
132	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
133	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
134	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
135	  app X := by
136	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
137	  naturality {X Y} f := by
138	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)
139	    dsimp
140	    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul,
141	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
142	    rfl
143	
144	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
145	/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
146	commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
147	noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
148	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
149	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
150	  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
151	  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
152	  μ_natural_left := by
153	    intro X Y f X'
154	    ext1 Z
155	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
156	      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
157	  μ_natural_right := by
158	    intro X Y X' f
159	    ext1 Z
160	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
161	      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
162	  associativity := by
163	    intro M N P
164	    ext1 Z
165	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
166	      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
167	  left_unitality := by
168	    intro M
169	    ext1 Z
170	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
171	      (M.obj Z)
172	  right_unitality := by
173	    intro M
174	    ext1 Z
175	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
176	      (M.obj Z)
177	
178	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
179	sheafification localizer
180	
181	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
182	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
183	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
184	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
185	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
186	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
187	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
188	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
189	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
190	structure. -/
191	
192	section FlatWhisker
193	
194	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
195	
196	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
197	
198	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
199	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
200	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
201	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
202	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
203	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
204	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
205	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
206	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
207	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
208	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
209	
210	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
211	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
212	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
213	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
214	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
215	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
216	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
217	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
218	
219	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
220	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
221	half. -/
222	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
223	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
224	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
225	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
226	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
227	  constructor
228	  intro U s
229	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
230	  | zero =>
231	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
232	      intro V i _
233	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
234	  | tmul a b =>
235	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
236	      intro V i hi
237	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
238	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
239	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
240	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
241	      congr 1
242	  | add s t hs ht =>
243	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
244	      intro V i hi
245	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
246	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
247	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
248	
249	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
250	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
251	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
252	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
253	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
254	injectivity half. -/
255	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
256	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
257	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
258	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
259	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
260	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
261	  constructor
262	  intro X ξ η h
263	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
264	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
265	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
266	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
267	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
268	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
269	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
270	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
271	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
272	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
273	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
274	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
275	  have hex : Function.Exact
276	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
277	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
278	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
279	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
280	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
281	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
282	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
283	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
284	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
285	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
286	    intro ζ
287	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
288	    | zero =>
289	        rw [map_zero]
290	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
291	    | tmul a kk =>
292	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
293	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
294	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
295	          rw [map_zero]
296	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
297	          exact kk.2
298	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
299	        intro V f hf
300	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
301	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
302	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
303	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
304	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
305	        erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl
306	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
307	        rw [map_add]
308	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
309	        intro V f hf
310	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
311	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
312	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
313	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
314	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
315	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
316	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
317	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
318	  intro V f hf
319	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
320	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
321	  exact hf
322	
323	/-- **Flat left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.**
324	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
325	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
326	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
327	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again
328	lies in `J.W`. The two halves are `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (where
329	flatness enters) and `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` (pure right-exactness).
330	This is the single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
331	`tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the route uses no `MonoidalClosed` structure. -/
332	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
333	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
334	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
335	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
336	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
337	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
338	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢
339	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1,
340	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2⟩
341	
342	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
343	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
344	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
345	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
346	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
347	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
348	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
349	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
350	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
351	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
352	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
353	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
354	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
355	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
356	  have hconj : g ▷ F
357	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
358	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
359	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
360	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
361	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
362	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
363	  exact hwl

365	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flatness-FREE whiskering of a locally
366	bijective morphism (ROUTE (d), the live associator realization)
367	
368	The flat whiskering above (`W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_flat`) needs the SECTIONWISE
369	flatness instance `∀ U, Module.Flat (R(U)) (F(U))`, which is FALSE for invertible
370	sheaves over non-affine opens and is therefore OFF the associator critical path
371	(iter-212 finding). The associator only ever whiskers the sheafification UNIT
372	`η = toSheafify`, which is **locally bijective** (`∈ J.W`), not merely locally
373	injective. Whiskering a *locally bijective* `g` by an *arbitrary* `F` preserves
374	local bijectivity with NO flatness hypothesis: stalkwise `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x}
375	⊗_{R_x} g_x`, and since `g_x` is an isomorphism (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso on
376	the topological site, `Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW` / `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints`), the
377	tensor `id ⊗ g_x` is again an isomorphism — no flatness, because *isomorphisms*
378	tensor to isomorphisms whereas mere *injections* need flatness. This is exactly the
379	flatness-free technique Mathlib blesses for `J.W.IsMonoidal` via enough points
380	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, route (d)). -/
381	
382	section WhiskerOfW
383	
384	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
385	
386	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
387	
388	/-- **Whiskering a locally bijective morphism preserves local injectivity
389	(flatness-free).** For an *arbitrary* presheaf of modules `F` and a morphism `g`
390	whose underlying additive-presheaf map is locally bijective (`∈ J.W`), the
391	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is locally injective.
392	
393	This is the single residual ingredient of the associator
394	`tensorObj_assoc_iso` under ROUTE (d). The mathematics is the stalkwise computation
395	`(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`: a `J.W`-morphism on the topological site of `X`
396	is a *stalkwise isomorphism* (`TopCat.hasEnoughPoints` + the conservative-family
397	characterisation `hP.W_iff`, `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.*`, 2026), and
398	tensoring an isomorphism `g_x` by `id_{F_x}` yields an isomorphism, so `F ◁ g` is a
399	stalkwise iso, hence locally bijective, hence locally injective — for *any* `F`,
400	needing NEITHER sectionwise flatness NOR local triviality of `F`.
401	
402	The two Mathlib-side ingredients this stalkwise argument needs are
403	(d.1) the stalkwise characterisation of the module-level `J.W` on `Opens X` and
404	(d.2) the commutation `(A ⊗ᵖ B)_x ≅ A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x` of the stalk with the
405	presheaf-of-modules tensor (stalk = filtered colimit, and `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight`
406	preserve filtered colimits over a module category). Neither ships at the
407	`PresheafOfModules` level at the pinned commit; porting them is the genuine residual
408	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, Decision 3, porting ingredients d.1/d.2). Stated
409	here with its substantive intended type so the rest of the associator closes against
410	it; see the task result for the precise missing Mathlib statements. -/
411	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
412	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
413	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
414	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
415	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
416	  -- ROUTE (e) residual: `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` is an iso since `g_x` is
417	  -- (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso); tensoring by `id` of an iso is flatness-free.
418	  --
419	  -- iter-214 CORRECTION (Step 0 + d.1 partial): the module-level stalk is NOT
420	  -- Mathlib-absent — `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk` supplies, for
421	  -- `X : TopCat`, `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module
422	  -- `Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` and `germ_smul`. The
423	  -- linear-stalk-map packaging (ingredient d.1) is now built project-side:
424	  -- `PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap` (+ `stalkLinearMap_germ`,
425	  -- `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`), all axiom-clean.
426	  --
427	  -- The TWO residual gaps to close this sorry (both require SPECIALISING this lemma
428	  -- to the topological site `C = Opens X` — the general-site statement here has no
429	  -- stalks; decl is UNPROTECTED so the specialisation is free, and the only consumer
430	  -- `tensorObj_assoc_iso` already works over `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`):
431	  --   (d.1-bridge) `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W (toPresheaf-image) ↔ ∀ x,
432	  --     IsIso (stalkFunctor Ab x map)` — assemble from `HasEnoughPoints
433	  --     (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)` (`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Points.lean`) +
434	  --     `W_iff`, OR from `WEqualsLocallyBijective` + the TopCat criteria
435	  --     `locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks` /
436	  --     `app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`
437	  --     (needs `presheafFiber ≅ TopCat.Presheaf.stalk`, the Mathlib TODO bridge).
438	  --   (d.2) stalk ⊗ commutation `(F ⊗ᵖ M)_x ≅ F_x ⊗_{R_x} M_x` naturally identifying
439	  --     `(F ◁ g)_x` with `LinearMap.lTensor F_x (stalkLinearMap g x)` — "tensor
440	  --     commutes with the filtered colimit defining the stalk" over the varying ring;
441	  --     genuinely Mathlib-absent (largest piece). Then `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`
442	  --     + `LinearEquiv.lTensor` finish (flatness-free). See task result for full decomp.
443	  sorry
444	
445	/-- **Flatness-free left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
446	ROUTE (d) replacement for `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for an *arbitrary* `F` and a
447	locally bijective `g` (`∈ J.W`), the left-whiskered `F ◁ g` again lies in `J.W`.
448	Local surjectivity is free (`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, right-exactness);
449	local injectivity is the flatness-free stalkwise residual
450	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. No flatness, no local triviality. -/
451	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
452	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
453	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
454	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
455	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
456	  have hbij := hg
457	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hbij
458	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
459	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg,
460	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hbij.2⟩
461	
462	/-- **Flatness-free right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
463	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`, mirroring `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`. -/
464	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
465	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
466	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
467	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
468	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
469	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg
470	  have hconj : g ▷ F
471	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
472	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
473	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
474	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
475	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
476	  exact hwl

478	end WhiskerOfW

480	/-- **The sheafification-localization bridge.** A morphism `f` of presheaves of
481	modules whose underlying additive-presheaf map lies in the sheafification localizer
482	`J.W` is sent by the associated-sheaf-of-modules functor to an isomorphism. This is
483	the single residual of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
484	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`. It is the morphism-property
485	identity `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms`
486	(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)
487	read at a single morphism. -/
488	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W
489	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
490	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
491	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
492	    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
493	    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
494	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
495	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
496	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
497	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf
498	  exact h2
499	
500	end FlatWhisker

502	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
503	(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)
504	
505	The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
506	argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
507	id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
508	characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
509	Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
510	as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.
511	
512	Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
513	and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
514	`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
515	scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
516	linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
517	This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
518	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
519	`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/
520	
521	section StalkLinearMap
522	
523	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
524	
525	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
526	
527	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
528	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
529	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
530	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
531	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
532	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
533	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
534	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
535	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
536	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
537	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
538	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
539	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
540	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
541	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
542	  map_smul' r s := by
543	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
544	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
545	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
546	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
547	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
548	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
549	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
550	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
551	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
552	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
553	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
554	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
555	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
556	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
557	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
558	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
559	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
560	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
561	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
562	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
563	      intro z
564	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
565	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
566	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
567	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
568	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
569	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
570	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
571	
572	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
573	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
574	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
575	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
576	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
577	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
578	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
579	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
580	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
581	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
582	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
583	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
584	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
585	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
586	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
587	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
588	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
589	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
590	
591	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
592	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
593	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
594	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
595	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
596	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
597	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
598	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
599	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
600	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
601	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
602	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
603	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
604	
605	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
606	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
607	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
608	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
609	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
610	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
611	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)

627	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
628	
629	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
630	
631	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
632	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
633	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
634	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
635	
636	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: the
637	body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body lifts
638	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
639	the small Zariski site of `X`. -/
640	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
641	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
642	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
643	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
644	
645	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
646	
647	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
648	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
649	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
650	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
651	
652	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. iter-202 Lane TS
653	scaffold: the body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body inherits the
654	morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
655	sheafification. -/
656	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
657	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
658	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
659	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
660	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
661	
662	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
663	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
664	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
665	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
666	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
667	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
668	law is definitional. -/
669	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
670	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
671	
672	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
673	
674	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
675	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
676	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
677	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
678	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
679	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
680	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
681	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
682	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
683	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
684	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/

686	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)

693	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
694	
695	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
696	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
697	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
698	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
699	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
700	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
701	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
702	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
703	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
704	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso

706	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
707	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))

846	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.

849	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`

851	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
852	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit

854	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
855	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
856	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
857	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
858	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
859	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
860	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
861	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
862	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
863	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
864	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
865	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
866	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
867	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
868	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
869	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
870	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
871	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
872	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
873	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
874	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
875	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
876	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
877	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI

879	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
880	
881	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
882	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
883

885	the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor) commutes
886	with the restriction functor `restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f` along an open
887	immersion. Mathematically it factors as: (i) the presheaf-of-modules tensor
888	commutes with the pullback of presheaves (sectionwise this is the
889	extension-of-scalars identity
890	`B ⊗_A (P ⊗_A Q) ≅ (B ⊗_A P) ⊗_B (B ⊗_A Q)` for `A → B`, Stacks 03DM); and
891	(ii) sheafification commutes with pullback along an open immersion (the
892	restriction is exact and the small-Zariski sheafification is local). Neither is
893	in Mathlib at the `SheafOfModules` level (there is no monoidal structure on
894	`SheafOfModules`, hence no strong-monoidal pullback). A genuine construction
895	requires either the `Localization.Monoidal` transport of the monoidal structure
896	through `PresheafOfModules.sheafification.IsLocalization` together with
897	`W.IsMonoidal` for `W := (J.W).inverseImage (toPresheaf _)` (an instance NOT
898	present in Mathlib and the real residual obstacle), or a direct sectionwise
899	construction. Left as a named typed `sorry` feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`;
900	see the task result for the precise missing instance statement. -/
901	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
902	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
903	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
904	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
905	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
906	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
907	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
908	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
909	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
910	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
911	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
912	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
913	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
914	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
915	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
916	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))

972	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
973	
974	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
975	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
976	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
977	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
978	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
979	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
980	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
981	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
982	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
983	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
984	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
985	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
986	  intro x
987	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
988	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
989	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
990	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
991	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
992	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
993	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
994	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
995	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
996	    tensorObj_unit_iso

1	\chapter{Relative Picard sheaf --- \texttt{Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} substrate (A.1.c.SubT)}
2	\label{chap:Picard_TensorObjSubstrate}
3	
4	\section{Motivation: the abelian-group-valued relative Picard sheaf}
5	\label{sec:tensorobj_motivation}
6	
7	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
8	\(k\). In Kleiman's framework (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor},
9	\cref{def:rel_pic_sharp}), the relative Picard functor
10	\[
11	  \Pic_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
12	  \qquad
13	  T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^* \Pic(T),
14	\]
15	is, by definition, abelian-group-valued. The abelian-group structure on each
16	fiber \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) comes from tensor product of line bundles
17	(Stacks tag 01CR / Hartshorne~II.\S 6), and the structure descends to the
18	quotient because \(\pi_T^*\) is a group homomorphism.
19	
20	% SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
21	% Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
22	% abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
23	% groups)
24	% (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
25	% SOURCE QUOTE:
26	% "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
27	%  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
28	%  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
29	% abelian groups defined by the formula
30	% 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
31	% \end{dfn}
32	%  [\dots]
33	% \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
34	%   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
35	% 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
36	%  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
37	% topologies  by
38	% 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
39	% 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
40	%  \end{dfn}"
41	\textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2, Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs.
42	The absolute and relative Picard functors take values in the category of
43	abelian groups; the relative functor is the quotient of one such group by
44	another, and the structure morphisms of the resulting presheaf are group
45	homomorphisms.}
46	
47	Concretely, the abelian-group law on \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the assignment
48	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr] :=
49	\bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
50	with neutral element the class of the structure sheaf
51	\(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) and inverse
52	\(- \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\), where
53	\(\mathcal{L}^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}}(\mathcal{L},
54	\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T})\) is the dual invertible sheaf. Pullback of
55	\(\mathcal{O}\)-modules along \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) preserves tensor
56	products and the structure sheaf, so the image
57	\(\pi_T^* \Pic(T) \subset \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a subgroup, and the quotient
58	\(\Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\) inherits a canonical abelian-group
59	structure. This is the content of
60	\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
61	
62	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
63	infrastructural, and --- crucially --- much smaller than a full
64	monoidal-category construction. The group law lives on \emph{isomorphism
65	classes} of line bundles: each group axiom is a \emph{proposition} asserting
66	the existence of an isomorphism (\(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\)),
67	and a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it. In
68	particular the coherence data of a monoidal category --- the pentagon,
69	triangle and hexagon identities, and a fortiori any closed structure --- is
70	\emph{never consumed} by a group law on iso-classes. What the law genuinely
71	requires on the carrier is therefore only:
72	\begin{enumerate}
73	  \item a binary tensor-product operation
74	    \(\otimes\, : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
75	                  \to \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), well-defined on
76	    isomorphism classes;
77	  \item the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as a designated unit object
78	    for \(\otimes\);
79	  \item the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
80	    (\(\exists N,\ M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)) together with the three
81	    monoidal coherence isomorphisms of \(\otimes_X\): associativity
82	    \((M \otimes N) \otimes P \cong M \otimes (N \otimes P)\), unit
83	    \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \cong M\) (and on the right), and commutativity
84	    \(M \otimes N \cong N \otimes M\). The inverse is carried by the
85	    predicate, not by a separate lemma.
86	\end{enumerate}
87	At Mathlib's pinned commit, the entire monoidal scaffolding for (3) is already
88	available abstractly, and the project's task is to \emph{instantiate} it rather
89	than to hand-assemble the coherence data. Two facts drive this. First, the
90	category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves of modules over a
91	presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) is already a (symmetric) monoidal category in
92	Mathlib (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\)), with the sectionwise
93	relative tensor \((M \otimes_R N)(U) = M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)\); this applies
94	verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf \(R = \mathcal{O}_X\). Second,
95	Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
96	(\(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)) takes a
97	monoidal category \(C\), a localization functor \(L : C \to D\) at a morphism
98	property \(W\), and an instance \(W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), and produces a
99	\emph{full} monoidal-category structure on the localization \(D\) --- associator,
100	left/right unitors, braiding, and \emph{all} coherence (pentagon, triangle,
101	naturalities) derived. Sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the
102	localization \(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class
103	\(W = J.W\), so the monoidal structure on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) is
104	\emph{free} from this API once \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) is supplied.
105	
106	Consequently the only genuinely-new obligation is the single instance
107	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) for the sheafification localizer on
108	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\). Its two fields are the
109	left/right whiskering-stability statements \(J.W\,g \Rightarrow J.W(F \triangleleft
110	g)\) and \(J.W\,g \Rightarrow J.W(g \triangleright F)\) for an \emph{arbitrary}
111	module \(F\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}), and these are flatness-free: a
112	\(J.W\)-morphism is a stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x =
113	\mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) is again an isomorphism (tensoring with an
114	isomorphism preserves isomorphisms --- no flatness, no local triviality). The
115	load-bearing half is the local-injectivity statement
116	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, whose stalkwise proof needs two
117	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\)-level ingredients that are Mathlib-absent at the
118	pinned commit (the module-level stalk characterisation of \(J.W\), and the
119	commutation of the stalk with the relative tensor); this is the
120	\emph{sole remaining open obligation} for the whole group-law engine.
121	
122	With \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) in hand, the substrate \(\otimes_X\) carries the
123	associator, unitors and braiding from \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\), and the
124	group law assembles the commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes whose units
125	group is the Picard group (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), exactly as
126	Mathlib builds \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) as
127	\(\mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\). The
128	\(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible},
129	Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) idiom) carries each inverse
130	existentially. Under route~(e), once \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) is in hand the
131	geometric compatibility isomorphism \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\)
132	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) is not needed for that path (the coherence
133	comes from the API); under the PRIMARY locally-trivial route, by contrast, it is
134	on the critical path (see \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, PRIMARY).
135	
136	\section{Mathlib API survey}
137	\label{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}
138	
139	The relevant pieces of the Mathlib API at the pinned commit
140	(\texttt{b80f227}) assemble into a single \emph{realization route} (route~(e)):
141	the monoidal structure on the substrate is obtained by instantiating Mathlib's
142	abstract localization-of-monoidal-categories API, not by hand-assembling the
143	associator. Four Mathlib facts are the formalization vehicle, all verified
144	against the on-disk Mathlib source.
145	
146	\paragraph{(i) The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category.} Mathlib's
147	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal} constructs a
148	monoidal-category instance
149	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\) on
150	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,(R \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,
151	\mathtt{RingCat})\), with the sectionwise relative tensor
152	\[
153	  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
154	\]
155	% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct` /
156	% `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, in
157	% `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean` (L104, L125)
158	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean).

168	% noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :
169	%     MonoidalCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where ..."
170	together with associator, left/right unitors and braiding making it symmetric
171	monoidal. The base presheaf of rings \(R \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,
172	\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat}\) is exactly the project's
173	\(\mathtt{Sheaf.val}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) carrier (equal by \(\mathtt{rfl}\)),
174	so this monoidal structure applies verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf

177	
178	\paragraph{(ii) The localization-of-monoidal-categories API.} Mathlib's
179	\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic} provides the class
180	\(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) and the construction
181	\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\). The class records exactly
182	the whiskering-stability data:
183	% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal` and
184	% `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal`, in
185	% `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Basic.lean` (L44, L86, L440)
186	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Basic.lean).
187	% SOURCE QUOTE:
188	% "class IsMonoidal : Prop extends W.IsMultiplicative where
189	%   whiskerLeft (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (hg : W g) : W (X ◁ g)
190	%   whiskerRight {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (hf : W f) (Y : C) : W (f ▷ Y)
191	%  ...
192	% def LocalizedMonoidal (L : C ⥤ D) (W : MorphismProperty C)
193	%     [W.IsMonoidal] [L.IsLocalization W] {unit : D} (_ : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit) := D
194	%  ...
195	% noncomputable instance :
196	%     MonoidalCategory (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) where ..."
197	Given \([\MonoidalCategory\,C]\), \([W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}]\), a localization
198	functor \(L : C \to D\) with \([L.\mathtt{IsLocalization}\,W]\) and an isomorphism
199	\(\varepsilon : L(\mathbf{1}_C) \cong \mathtt{unit}\), the localized category
200	\(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) is a \(\MonoidalCategory\) with
201	\emph{all} coherence (associator, unitors, pentagon, triangle, naturalities)
202	derived. There is \emph{no} \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) anywhere in the
203	hypothesis chain.
204	
205	\paragraph{(iii) The proof template for $W$-monoidality.} Mathlib's
206	\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW} proves
207	\((J.W\,(A := A)).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) \emph{stalkwise} for presheaves valued in a
208	\emph{fixed} monoidal concrete category \(A\) (i.e.\ \(C^{op} \to A\)), via the
209	conservative-family characterisation \(\mathtt{hP.W\_iff}\),
210	\(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.map\_tensor}\) and typeclass inference:
211	% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `CategoryTheory.ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W`
212	% and the `(J.W).IsMonoidal` instance, in
213	% `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean` (L48, L57)
214	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean).
215	% SOURCE QUOTE:
216	% "lemma ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W
217	%     [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] :
218	%     (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal :=
219	%   .mk' _ (fun f g hf hg ↦ by
220	%     simp only [hP.W_iff (A := A)] at hf hg ⊢
221	%     intro Φ
222	%     rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor]
223	%     infer_instance)"
224	This is the \emph{template} for the proof technique used in route~(e), but it does
225	\emph{not} apply directly to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\): modules over a
226	\emph{varying} ring are not of the form \(C^{op} \to A\) for a fixed monoidal
227	\(A\).
228	
229	\paragraph{The gap and route~(e).} The substrate
230	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) of this chapter is the
231	sheafification of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\), and
232	sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the localization
233	\(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\). Hence
234	the monoidal structure on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X =
235	\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) is obtained by applying
236	\(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) (ii) to the already-monoidal category (i) ---
237	\emph{once} the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) is supplied. That instance is
238	the \emph{sole genuinely-new obligation}: its two whiskering-stability fields are
239	\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}, whose load-bearing local-injectivity half is
240	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, the only remaining open sorry. The
241	proof technique is that of template (iii) ported to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\):
242	a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a stalkwise isomorphism, so
243	\((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) is again an isomorphism
244	for \emph{any} \(F\) --- no flatness, no local triviality.
245	
246	What is \emph{genuinely Mathlib-absent} at the pinned commit is narrower than a
247	total absence of stalk infrastructure. The \emph{stalk module structure} is
248	already present in Mathlib: the file
249	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk} supplies, for a topological
250	space \(X\), a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) on \(X\) and a point
251	\(x \in X\), the \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-module structure on the stalk
252	\(\mathtt{stalk}\,M.\mathtt{presheaf}\,x\) of a presheaf of modules \(M\),
253	together with the germ--scalar compatibility \(\mathtt{germ\_smul}\). What was
254	Mathlib-absent is only the \emph{linearity packaging} of the \emph{induced stalk
255	map} of a morphism of presheaves of modules --- that the map on stalks is
256	\(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear, characterised on germs, bijective when the
257	underlying \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-stalk map is an isomorphism, and bundled as a linear
258	equivalence. This packaging is now built project-side as the four declarations of
259	\cref{lem:stalk_linear_map} (the \emph{linearity half} of ingredient (d.1)). The
260	pieces that remain genuinely Mathlib-absent are: there is no monoidal
261	\(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\); the (d.1)-\emph{bridge} between the site-level
262	\(J.W\) and the topological stalkwise-isomorphism criterion on
263	\(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\); and (d.2) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
264	\otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk with the relative module-presheaf tensor. So
265	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) for the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) sheafification
266	localizer must still be built project-side, and its two residual stalk-level
267	ingredients --- the (d.1)-bridge and (d.2) --- are ported by
268	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. Once \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\)
269	lands, the associator, unitors and braiding are \emph{free} from (ii) and the
270	bespoke three-step composite is superseded.
271	
272	The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
273	built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
274	\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
275	and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
276	PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
277	project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
278	independently of any upstream timing.

280	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
281	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
282	
283	\begin{definition}
284	
285	\leanok
286	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
287	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
288	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
289	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
290	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
291	  \[
292	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
293	  \]
294	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
295	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
296	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
297	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
298	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
299	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
300	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
301	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
302	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
303	  canonical compatibility
304	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
305	     \;\cong\;
306	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
307	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
308	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
309	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
310	
311	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
312	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
313	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
314	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
315	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
316	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
317	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
318	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
319	\end{definition}
320	
321	\begin{lemma}
322	
323	\leanok
324	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
325	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
326	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
327	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
328	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
329	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
330	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
331	  \[
332	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
333	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
334	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
335	  \]
336	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
337	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
338	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
339	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
340	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
341	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
342	  \[
343	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
344	    \qquad
345	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
346	  \]
347	  the associator
348	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
349	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
350	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
351	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
352	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
353	\end{lemma}
354	
355	\begin{proof}
356	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
357	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
358	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
359	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
360	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
361	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
362	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
363	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
364	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
365	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
366	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
367	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
368	\end{proof}
369	
370	\begin{remark}
371	[The full monoidal category is cheap via route~(e); the group law consumes only propositions]
372	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
373	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
374	        lem:whisker_of_W}
375	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
376	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
377	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
378	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
379	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
380	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. Under route~(e) this is
381	  \emph{cheap}, not expensive: it is exactly the output of
382	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
383	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
384	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), once the single instance
385	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}) is supplied. All the
386	  coherence is then derived by the API.
387	
388	  Crucially, \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) needs \emph{no} closed structure. Its
389	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
390	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
391	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}: a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
392	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
393	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\) (tensoring with an isomorphism
394	  preserves isomorphisms). This is the same technique Mathlib uses for
395	  fixed-base presheaves in
396	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\), ported to
397	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\). It uses neither
398	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
399	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
400	  false over a non-affine open and is therefore off the critical path); the
401	  earlier reading that arbitrary-\(F\) whisker stability requires
402	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) was the \emph{wrong altitude}.
403	
404	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
405	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
406	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
407	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
408	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
409	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
410	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
411	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
412	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
413	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, all now supplied by the route~(e) monoidal structure;
414	  under route~(e) none of them is built from the restriction-compatibility
415	  isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (which is instead on the critical
416	  path of the PRIMARY locally-trivial route, via
417	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}). This mirrors Mathlib's own Picard group
418	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}),
419	  whose \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) is transported from
420	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\): there
421	  over a fixed ring, here over the sheafification localizer via
422	  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\).
423	\end{remark}
424	
425	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
426	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
427	
428	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
429	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
430	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
431	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
432	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the
433	\emph{existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity, unit,
434	commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law consumes ---
435	under route~(e) these are read off the \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure of
436	\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} --- together with the closure of \(\otimes\) on the
437	locally-trivial \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype
438	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and the interaction with the pullback
439	functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
440	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is on the critical path of the PRIMARY
441	locally-trivial group law (it is consumed by the PRIMARY proof of
442	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}); it is dispensable only under the
443	route~(e) stalkwise fallback.
444	
445	\begin{lemma}
446	
447	\leanok
448	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
449	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
450	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
451	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
452	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
453	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
454	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
455	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
456	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
457	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
458	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
459	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
460	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
461	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
462	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
463	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
464	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
465	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
466	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
467	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
468	  \[
469	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
470	      \;\;:=\;\;
471	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
472	  \]
473	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
474	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
475	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
476	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
477	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
478	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
479	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
480	\end{lemma}
481	
482	\begin{proof}
483	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
484	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
485	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
486	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
487	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
488	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
489	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
490	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
491	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
492	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
493	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
494	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
495	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
496	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
497	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
498	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
499	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
500	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
501	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
502	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a defeq
503	  transport across the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
504	
505	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
506	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
507	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
508	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
509	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
510	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
511	\end{proof}
512	
513	\begin{lemma}
514	
515	\leanok
516	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
517	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
518	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
519	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
520	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
521	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
522	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
523	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
524	  morphism
525	  \[
526	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
527	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
528	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
529	  \]
530	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
531	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
532	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
533	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
534	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
535	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
536	\end{lemma}
537	
538	\begin{proof}
539	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
540	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
541	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
542	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
543	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
544	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
545	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
546	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
547	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
548	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
549	  isomorphism}
550	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
551	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
552	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
553	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
554	  therefore extension of scalars along
555	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
556	
557	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
558	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
559	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
560	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
561	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
562	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
563	  the abstract pullback functor.
564	
565	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
566	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
567	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
568	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
569	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
570	   \;\cong\;
571	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
572	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
573	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
574	  comparison
575	  \[
576	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
577	      \;\longrightarrow\;
578	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
579	      \;\otimes\;
580	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
581	  \]
582	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
583	
584	  \emph{Step 3 (the genuine residual).} Completing this comparison at the
585	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise description of the abstract functor
586	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
587	  adjoint of pushforward and admits no sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the
588	  pinned commit. The genuine residual decomposes into two Mathlib-absent
589	  presheaf-level ingredients --- a presheaf-level identification
590	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) via
591	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) (needing a presheaf-level pushforward
592	  adjunction, only the sheaf version of which exists), and a strong-monoidal
593	  upgrade of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the ring isomorphism
594	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\). Under the PRIMARY locally-trivial group law this lemma
595	  is \emph{on} the critical path: the locally-trivial proof of
596	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} reduces local injectivity of
597	  \(F \triangleleft g\) on a trivialising cover \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\)
598	  through exactly this restriction-compatibility comparison (together with the
599	  left unitor). Closing Step 3 is therefore a primary obligation, not an optional
600	  supplement. (Only under the route-(e) fallback, where the whiskering field is
601	  proved stalkwise for arbitrary \(F\) via (d.2), would restriction-compatibility
602	  be unnecessary.)
603	\end{proof}
604	
605	\begin{lemma}
606	
607	\leanok
608	[Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial]
609	  \label{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
610	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial}
611	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
612	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, and let
613	  \(L, L' \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be locally-trivial modules of
614	  rank one (i.e.\ line bundles). The tensor product \(L \otimes_X L'\) of
615	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is again locally trivial of rank
616	  one. Consequently the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) restricts to a bifunctor
617	  on the full subcategory \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X \subset \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
618	  of locally-trivial rank-one modules.
619	\end{lemma}
620	
621	\begin{proof}
622	  Pick a common affine open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) on which both
623	  \(L|_{U_i}\) and \(L'|_{U_i}\) are free of rank one. On each \(U_i\)
624	  there are trivialisations \(L|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and
625	  \(L'|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). The tensor product of two free
626	  rank-one modules over a commutative ring is again free of rank one;
627	  hence \((L \otimes_X L')|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). So
628	  \(L \otimes_X L'\) is locally trivial of rank one, i.e.\ an object of
629	  \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X\).
630	\end{proof}
