632	\begin{lemma}
633	
634	[Flat whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer]
635	  \label{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}
636	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_flat, PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_flat}
637	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
638	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a Grothendieck
639	  topology \(J\), and let \(J.W\) be the class of morphisms of presheaves of
640	  \(R\)-modules that are inverted by sheafification --- equivalently, the
641	  \emph{locally bijective} morphisms, i.e.\ those that are both locally injective
642	  and locally surjective for \(J\) (the characterisation
643	  \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\)). Let \(F\) be a presheaf of \(R\)-modules
644	  that is \emph{flat} (sectionwise: \(F(U)\) is a flat \(R(U)\)-module for every
645	  object \(U\) of the site). Then for every morphism \(g\) in \(J.W\) the
646	  left-whiskered morphism
647	  \[
648	    F \triangleleft g \;\;=\;\; \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g
649	  \]
650	  (the action of \(F \otimes^{p} -\) on \(g\), with \(\otimes^{p}\) the
651	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product) again lies in \(J.W\). By the symmetry of
652	  the presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, the right-whiskered morphism
653	  \(g \triangleright F\) likewise lies in \(J.W\) when \(F\) is flat.
654	
655	  Both halves --- the left-whiskering statement and its braiding-conjugate
656	  right-whiskering statement --- are established. They are valid standalone
657	  results about flat whiskering of sheafification-localizer morphisms. They are,
658	  however, \emph{off the critical path}: the live whiskering-stability data for
659	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (route~(e)) are the flatness-free variants
660	  \(\mathtt{W\_whiskerLeft\_of\_W}\) / \(\mathtt{W\_whiskerRight\_of\_W}\)
661	  (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}), whose stalkwise proof holds for an \emph{arbitrary}
662	  whiskering object \(F\) and never invokes the sectionwise-flatness hypothesis
663	  of the present lemma. The sectionwise flatness condition --- \(F(U)\) flat over
664	  \(R(U)\) for \emph{every} object \(U\) of the site, including non-affine opens
665	  --- is not supplied by \(\otimes\)-invertibility or local triviality, which are
666	  affine-local properties; the present (flat) lemma is therefore retained as an
667	  independent, closed result, superseded on the critical path by the
668	  \(\mathtt{\_of\_W}\) variants of \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}.
669	\end{lemma}
670	
671	\begin{proof}
672	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
673	  By the locally-bijective characterisation of the sheafification localizer
674	  (\(J.W\) is exactly the class of morphisms that are \(J\)-locally injective and
675	  \(J\)-locally surjective), it suffices to show that \(F \triangleleft g\) is
676	  both locally injective and locally surjective whenever \(g\) is.
677	
678	  The presheaf-of-modules tensor product is computed sectionwise,
679	  \((F \otimes^{p} G)(U) = F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} G(U)\), so for
680	  \(g : A \to B\) the whiskered morphism acts on sections over \(U\) as the
681	  \(R(U)\)-linear map
682	  \[
683	    \mathrm{id}_{F(U)} \otimes g(U) \;\colon\;
684	      F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} A(U) \;\longrightarrow\; F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} B(U).
685	  \]
686	
687	  \emph{Local surjectivity} is automatic and needs no hypothesis on \(F\):
688	  tensoring is right exact, so applying \(F \otimes^{p} -\) carries the cokernel
689	  of \(g\) to the cokernel of \(F \triangleleft g\); since \(g\) is locally
690	  surjective its cokernel is locally zero, hence so is the cokernel of
691	  \(F \triangleleft g\), and \(F \triangleleft g\) is locally surjective.
692	
693	  \emph{Local injectivity} is where flatness enters. Because each \(F(U)\) is a
694	  flat \(R(U)\)-module, the functor \(F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} -\) preserves injective
695	  linear maps (Mathlib
696	  \(\mathtt{Module.Flat.lTensor\_preserves\_injective\_linearMap}\)). Thus
697	  whenever \(g(U)\) is injective on a covering sieve, so is
698	  \((\mathrm{id}_{F(U)} \otimes g(U))\); the local injectivity of \(g\) is
699	  therefore transported sectionwise to \(F \triangleleft g\).
700	
701	  Locally injective and locally surjective together give
702	  \(F \triangleleft g \in J.W\). The right-whiskered statement
703	  \(g \triangleright F\) follows by conjugating \(F \triangleleft g\) with the
704	  braiding of the symmetric presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, which is an
705	  isomorphism and hence preserves membership in \(J.W\); the flatness of \(F\) is
706	  the only input, and it is symmetric in the two tensor factors.
707	\end{proof}

718	  map of presheaves of rings. Let \(f : A \to B\) be a morphism of presheaves of
719	  \(R_0\)-modules whose underlying morphism of presheaves of abelian groups
720	  \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0).\mathrm{map}\,f\) lies in the sheafification
721	  localizer class \(J.W\) --- equivalently, is locally bijective for \(J\). Then
722	  the presheaf-of-modules sheafification functor

737	  sheafification of presheaves of \(R_0\)-modules \emph{is} the localization of

739	  \(J.W.\mathtt{inverseImage}\,(\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0)\) of morphisms whose
740	  underlying map of presheaves of abelian groups is locally bijective. Concretely,
741	  the underlying \(\mathtt{AddCommGrp}\)-valued sheafification inverts exactly the
742	  locally bijective morphisms, and Mathlib's
743	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.inverseImage\_W\_toPresheaf\_eq\_inverseImage\_%
744	  isomorphisms}\) identifies the preimage of \(J.W\) under
745	  \(\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0\) with the preimage of the class of isomorphisms under
746	  the module-level sheafification functor. Reading that equality of morphism
747	  classes at the single morphism \(f\): the hypothesis
748	  \(J.W\,\bigl((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0).\mathrm{map}\,f\bigr)\) places \(f\) in
749	  that preimage, hence \((\mathtt{sheafification}\,\alpha).\mathrm{map}\,f\) is an
750	  isomorphism. No new construction is required beyond instantiating the existing
751	  Mathlib identity at one morphism; this lemma is a closed thin wrapper, retained

755	\section{Route~(e): the \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal structure}
756	\label{sec:tensorobj_route_e}
757	
758	The monoidal structure on the substrate is realized by \emph{instantiating}
759	Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
760	(\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}): apply
761	\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) to the already-monoidal
762	presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) and the
763	sheafification localizer \(J.W\). The single new input the API requires is the
764	instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), whose two fields are the
765	whiskering-stability lemmas below.
766	
767	\paragraph{Two-tier strategy.} The group law is assembled by a \emph{two-tier}
768	strategy, of which this route~(e) is the second tier. The \textbf{primary} tier
769	builds the commutative group directly on isomorphism classes of \emph{locally
770	trivial} (invertible) sheaves (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), mirroring
771	Mathlib's ring-level Picard group \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) /
772	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) (\texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean}). On top of
773	the locally-trivial-scoped associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, the unitors
774	\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso} (all
775	either already proved or locally-trivial-scoped and assembled), it needs only the
776	restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} and the
777	dual/inverse \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}; it uses \emph{no}
778	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), \emph{no} \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\), \emph{no}
779	\(\mathtt{Skeleton}\), and in particular never invokes the stalk--tensor commutation
780	(d.2). The \textbf{fallback} tier is the full \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to
781	\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal category on all of \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\)
782	(this section); it is deferred because its load-bearing whiskering field
783	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, for an arbitrary whisker object, needs
784	(d.2) --- the stalk commuting with the relative tensor over a \emph{varying} ring, a
785	Mathlib-absent ingredient --- and is retained only for the full
786	monoidal generality on arbitrary modules, which the group law does not consume. The
787	load-bearing local-injectivity half of \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) is thus the sole
788	remaining open obligation of the \emph{fallback} tier only.
789

792	[The $R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x$-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules]
793	  \label{lem:stalk_linear_map}
794	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap, PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap_germ, PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso, PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso}
795	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
796	  Let \(X\) be a topological space, \(R\) a presheaf of commutative rings on
797	  \(X\), and \(x \in X\) a point. For a morphism \(g : M \to N\) of presheaves of
798	  \(R\)-modules, the induced map on stalks
799	  \[
800	    g_x \;\colon\; M_x \;\longrightarrow\; N_x
801	  \]
802	  is \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap}\)), and is
803	  characterised on germs by
804	  \(g_x(\mathrm{germ}_x\,s) = \mathrm{germ}_x(g_U\,s)\)
805	  (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap\_germ}\)). When the underlying map of
806	  \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-valued stalks is an isomorphism, \(g_x\) is bijective
807	  (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap\_bijective\_of\_isIso}\)); in that case it bundles to
808	  an \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear equivalence
809	  \(M_x \xrightarrow{\sim} N_x\) (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso}\)).

811	  These four declarations are the \emph{(d.1)-partial implementation} --- the
812	  linearity half of ingredient (d.1) of
813	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} --- consumed by the

815	  stalk module structure of \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk};

821	  The stalk \(M_x = \mathtt{stalk}\,M.\mathtt{presheaf}\,x\) carries its
822	  \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-module structure from
823	  \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk}. The underlying additive map
824	  \(g_x\) on stalks is the filtered colimit of the section maps \(g_U\); since

872	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:stalk_linear_map,
873	        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}

875	  \emph{PRIMARY route (\(F\) locally trivial --- the consumer's case; sheaf-level,
876	  no stalks, no (d.2)).} Suppose \(F\) is locally trivial
877	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,F\)), which is exactly the hypothesis its
878	  sole consumer --- the associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} --- supplies.
879	  \(J\)-local injectivity of a morphism is \emph{local on a covering family}: it
880	  suffices to produce a cover of \(X\) on each member of which \(F \triangleleft g\)
881	  restricts to a locally injective morphism. Take a trivialising open cover
882	  \(\{V\}\) of \(X\) for \(F\), so that \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\) on each \(V\).
883	  On such a \(V\):
884	  \begin{itemize}
885	    \item the substrate--restriction compatibility \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
886	      gives a canonical isomorphism
887	      \[

889	      \]

894	    \item the trivialisation \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\) together with the left
895	      unitor \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} (\(\mathcal{O}_V \otimes_V (-) \cong

901	  injective. An isomorphism preserves local injectivity, hence \((F \triangleleft
902	  g)|_V\) is locally injective on \(V\). As the \(\{V\}\) cover \(X\) and local
903	  injectivity is local on the cover, \(F \triangleleft g\) is \(J\)-locally
904	  injective. \emph{No stalks and no (d.2) stalk--tensor commutation enter}; the

906	  comparison, decomposed into its two open-immersion base-change halves) carries the

913	  morphism); a mere injection is never tensored, so no flatness is needed.

924	  the stalk (a filtered colimit), giving

933	  locally bijective, in particular locally injective, for \emph{any} \(F\).

980	\begin{lemma}
981	[Flatness-free whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer]
982	  \label{lem:whisker_of_W}
983	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W, PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W}
984	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
985	  With notation as in \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}: for an
986	  \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of \(R\)-modules \(F\) and a morphism \(g\) whose
987	  underlying additive-presheaf map lies in \(J.W\), both whiskerings again lie in
988	  \(J.W\):
989	  \[
990	    g \in J.W \;\Longrightarrow\; F \triangleleft g \in J.W,
991	    \qquad
992	    g \in J.W \;\Longrightarrow\; g \triangleright F \in J.W .
993	  \]
994	  These two statements are precisely the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) and
995	  \(\mathtt{whiskerRight}\) data required by the class
996	  \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}) for
997	  the morphism property \(J.W\); together they furnish the instance
998	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) of \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}. No flatness and no
999	  local triviality is used --- they are the flatness-free replacements for the
1000	  flat variants of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}.
1001	\end{lemma}
1002	
1003	\begin{proof}
1004	  \uses{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
1005	  By the locally-bijective characterisation of \(J.W\), it suffices to show
1006	  \(F \triangleleft g\) is both locally injective and locally surjective.
1007	  \emph{Local surjectivity} is free and needs no hypothesis on \(F\): the presheaf
1008	  tensor is right exact, so \(F \otimes^{p} -\) carries the locally-zero cokernel
1009	  of \(g\) to the cokernel of \(F \triangleleft g\), which is therefore locally
1010	  zero (Mathlib \(\mathtt{isLocallySurjective\_whiskerLeft}\)). \emph{Local
1011	  injectivity} is \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. The two together
1012	  give \(F \triangleleft g \in J.W\). The right-whiskered statement
1013	  \(g \triangleright F\) follows by conjugating \(F \triangleleft g\) with the
1014	  braiding of the symmetric presheaf monoidal structure, which is an isomorphism
1015	  and hence preserves membership in \(J.W\).
1016	\end{proof}
1017	
1018	\begin{lemma}
1019	[The \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal substrate (route~(e) target)]
1020	  \label{lem:jw_ismonoidal}
1021	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:whisker_of_W}
1022	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The sheafification localizer \(J.W\) on
1023	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) is \emph{monoidal}: it carries an
1024	  instance
1025	  \[
1026	    J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal},
1027	  \]
1028	  whose two fields are the whiskering-stability lemmas \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}
1029	  (and which is multiplicative because sheafification inverts identities and
1030	  composites). Consequently, by Mathlib's
1031	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)
1032	  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}) applied to the already-monoidal category
1033	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\), the localization functor
1034	  \(L = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) (which is the localization at
1035	  \(J.W\)), and the unit isomorphism \(L(\mathbf{1}) \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), the
1036	  category
1037	  \[
1038	    \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
1039	      \;=\; \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}
1040	  \]
1041	  acquires a full \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure whose tensor is the substrate
1042	  \(\otimes_X\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}) and whose associator,
1043	  left/right unitors, braiding and \emph{all} coherence (pentagon, triangle,
1044	  naturalities) are derived by the API. This is the route~(e) target: the
1045	  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, the unitors
1046	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso} are
1047	  then components of this derived monoidal structure rather than hand-assembled
1048	  composites.
1049	
1050	  This block states the route~(e) construction; it is currently
1051	  \emph{stated-but-unformalized} (no Lean declaration realizes the
1052	  \(\MonoidalCategory\) instance yet), pending the sole open obligation
1053	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. It mirrors Mathlib's
1054	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) construction one categorical level up.
1055	\end{lemma}
1056	
1057	\begin{proof}
1058	  \uses{lem:whisker_of_W}
1059	  Multiplicativity of \(J.W\) (closure under identities and composition) is part
1060	  of its being a localizer (\(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\)). The two
1061	  whiskering-stability fields of \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) are
1062	  exactly the left- and right-whiskering statements of \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}.
1063	  Hence \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) holds. Sheafification of presheaves of
1064	  modules is a localization at \(J.W\) (the underlying identification of
1065	  sheafification with localization at the locally-bijective class), and the unit
1066	  presheaf sheafifies to \(\mathcal{O}_X\); feeding these to
1067	  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) produces the \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
1068	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with all coherence derived, as in
1069	  \cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}. No \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure enters
1070	  at any point.
1071	\end{proof}

1076	[Associator for $\otimes_X$ on locally trivial objects]

1079	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:jw_ismonoidal}
1080	  % NOTE: under route~(e) the associator is the API-derived associator of the

1088	  \[

1097	  isomorphism, the proposition \(\mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X
1098	  P \cong M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\bigr)\).

1100	  The current Lean pin additionally carries \emph{locally trivial}
1101	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\), \cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial})
1102	  hypotheses on \(M, N, P\); under route~(e) these are \emph{vestigial} --- they

1115	  needed. Reading \(\alpha_{M,N,P}\) at the three fixed objects gives the

1301	  % \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules (Stacks \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} /
1302	  % \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}), independently of the open-immersion
1303	  % restriction-compatibility iso.

1305	  functor
1306	  \[
1307	    \pi_T^{*} \;\;\colon\;\; \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \;\longrightarrow\;
1308	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1309	  \]
1310	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
1311	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
1312	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
1313	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
1314	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
1315	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a

1327	  the standard fact that the extension of scalars

1340	\section{\(\otimes\)-invertibility and the commutative-monoid group-law engine}
1341	\label{sec:tensorobj_invertibility}
1342	
1343	The relative Picard group law is assembled from \emph{\(\otimes\)-invertibility}
1344	and the monoidal coherence isomorphisms of
1345	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1346	following Mathlib's Picard-group idiom (\(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\),
1347	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic} = \mathrm{Units}(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R))\),
1348	\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\); \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) as the
1349	design template. Under the route~(e) fallback the inverse of an invertible object
1350	is carried by the predicate itself, so neither the open-immersion
1351	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} nor a
1352	separate tensor-inverse lemma is needed there. Under the PRIMARY locally-trivial
1353	route, by contrast, both \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (via the PRIMARY proof
1354	of \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}) and the tensor-inverse lemma
1355	\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} (supplying inverses to
1356	\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) are on the critical path.
1357

1359	[\(\otimes\)-invertibility of an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module]
1360	  \label{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}

1362	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}

1365	  % (lemma-invertible, the "$\exists\,\mathcal{N}$" characterisation)
1366	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4046-L4079).
1367	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1368	  % "\begin{definition}

1370	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. An
1371	  % {\it invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module} is a sheaf
1372	  % of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules $\mathcal{L}$ such that
1373	  % the functor
1374	  % $$
1375	  % \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \longrightarrow \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X),\quad
1376	  % \mathcal{F} \longmapsto \mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{F}
1377	  % $$

1379	  % {\it trivial} if it is isomorphic as an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module

# `tensorObj_restrict_iso` — corrected decomposition (iter-208 Lane TS, Route A audit)

## Target

```lean
noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)
```
where `tensorObj M N := (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
(PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`.

## State in the file (compiles GREEN, after iter-208)

The proof now performs **three real reduction steps** before the residual `sorry`:

1. `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f` — reduce `·.restrict f` to
   `(pullback f).obj ·` (sheaf-level pullback). [Mathlib]
2. `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom` — move the
   pullback inside the sheafification. [Mathlib]
3. **(NEW iter-208)** `(PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_`
   — strip the OUTER sheafification: both sides are `sheafification.obj _`, so it
   suffices to give the presheaf-level comparison. VERIFIED: resulting goal has no
   sheafification.

After step 3 the residual is the **purely presheaf-level**
```
(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
  ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val
```
with `φ.hom = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom`, `⊗ₚ = PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`.

## The Route-A recipe (`analogies/tsroute208.md`) is INCORRECT on the key point

The recipe says the residual is a "~30–60 LOC sectionwise unfolding of
`PresheafOfModules.pullback φ` along the open immersion", to be discharged
"sectionwise via `isoMk` ... over each `U` both sides are `M(f''U) ⊗ N(f''U)` by
`restrict_obj` `rfl`, differing only by the scalar ring".

This conflates two DIFFERENT functors:
- `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom` (what appears in the residual) is the
  **OPAQUE abstract left adjoint** `(pushforward φ.hom).leftAdjoint`
  (`Mathlib/.../Presheaf/Pullback.lean:44`, built via the `leftAdjointObjIsDefined`
  partial-adjoint machinery). It has **NO sectionwise formula**. The `restrict_obj`
  `rfl` the recipe cites is a fact about **`restrict`/`pushforward`**, not
  `pullback`: `M.restrict f = Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor f =
  SheafOfModules.pushforward β` (a CONCRETE sectionwise pushforward along
  `f.opensFunctor`, `Modules/Sheaf.lean`), whose `.val` is
  `(PresheafOfModules.pushforward β.hom).obj M.val`.
- The kaehler precedent the recipe cites (`analogies/kaehler-...presheafpullback`,
  Decision 5) hit this EXACT opacity and **excised** its pullback-unfolding helper
  (`Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` iter-145) — it was never built.

So there is no cheap sectionwise unfolding of the opaque `pullback`. The residual
must be attacked by relating `pullback φ.hom` to the concrete `pushforward β.hom`.

## Genuine route: TWO project-side ingredients, BOTH absent from Mathlib

### H1 (the linchpin) — presheaf-level `pushforward β.hom ≅ pullback φ.hom`

`pullback φ.hom := (pushforward φ.hom).leftAdjoint`. Exhibit a presheaf-level
adjunction `PresheafOfModules.pushforward β.hom ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom`
and conclude `pushforward β.hom ≅ pullback φ.hom` by `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`
(against `PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.hom`).

- `β.hom` is the structure map of `restrictFunctor f`: from `Modules/Sheaf.lean`,
  `restrictFunctor f = SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
  ⟨whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩` with `α.app U = (f.appIso U.unop).inv`.
  Hence `(M.restrict f).val = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β.hom).obj M.val`
  DEFINITIONALLY.
- The adjunction is the **presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level**
  `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj`
  (`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:226`), built from the opens-functor
  adjunction `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`
  (`f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction`) plus the two compatibility squares
  `H₁`, `H₂`. The unit/counit have the simple sectionwise form
  `M.val.map (adj.counit/unit.app U).op`.
- **Absent Mathlib building blocks** (only the SHEAF versions exist): presheaf-level
  `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans` and `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr`.
  `pushforwardId`/`pushforwardComp` DO exist at presheaf level.
- Estimate: ~100–150 LOC (mirror `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` + the 2 helpers +
  verify `H₁`/`H₂` for the open-immersion data).

### H2 — strong-monoidal comparison for `pushforward β.hom`

`(pushforward β.hom).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅ (pushforward β.hom).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β.hom).obj B`.

- `PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ`
  (`Presheaf/Pushforward.lean:86`). `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` is already
  `Monoidal` (`PushforwardZeroMonoidal.lean:33`, `μIso = refl`). So H2 reduces to
  upgrading the file's lax `PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal` (already
  present, lines 147–177) to **STRONG** when `α` is componentwise a ring iso, via
  `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal` (`Monoidal/Functor.lean:718`, needs
  `IsIso (ε F)` and `∀ X Y, IsIso (μ F X Y)`). Then
  `(pushforward β.hom).Monoidal := (pushforward₀OfCommRingCat).Monoidal.comp
  (restrictScalars β-part).Monoidal` and H2 is its `μIso`.
- `IsIso ε`: EASY — sectionwise `ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)` has
  underlying map the ring map `α.app X` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η`), iso since
  `α` is a NatIso.
- `IsIso μ`: the REAL bottom gap. Sectionwise this is "`ModuleCat.restrictScalars`
  along a ring isomorphism is strong monoidal" — i.e. the tensorator
  `restrictScalars(M₁) ⊗_R restrictScalars(M₂) → restrictScalars(M₁ ⊗_S M₂)`
  (`m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂ ↦ m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂`, `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul`) is iso for a ring
  iso `R ≃+* S`. **Absent from Mathlib**: `ModuleCat.extendScalars` is `Monoidal`
  (`Monoidal/Adjunction.lean:42`) but `restrictScalars` is only `LaxMonoidal`
  (`:102`). Provable (~30–50 LOC) either by a direct `TensorProduct` `LinearEquiv`
  (base change along a ring iso) or by transporting `extendScalars`-monoidality
  across the adjoint equivalence `extendScalars e ⊣ restrictScalars e` (for `e`
  iso both are equivalences, so `restrictScalars e ≅ extendScalars e.symm`).
- Then lift the sectionwise `IsIso μ` to the presheaf `μ` (the file's
  `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) via a presheaf-of-modules iso-from-sectionwise criterion
  (`PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf` reflects isos + `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`).
- Estimate: ~60–100 LOC.

### Closure once H1, H2 land

```
(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
  ≅[H1.symm]  (pushforward β.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
  ≅[H2]       (pushforward β.hom M.val) ⊗ₚ (pushforward β.hom N.val)
  =defeq      (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val
```

## Verdict

This is a **~200–300 LOC, multi-ingredient `mathlib-build`-scale** task, NOT a
single prove-mode close. The four absent Mathlib ingredients are:
1. `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans` (presheaf-level).
2. `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr` (presheaf-level).
3. presheaf-level `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (gives H1).
4. strong-monoidal `ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring iso (gives the H2 bottom).

The informal-agent consult was unavailable this iter: the only key present
(`MOONSHOT_API_KEY`) is a "Kimi For Coding" subscription key, rejected by the
chat-completions API (403 `access_terminated_error` on `api.kimi.com/coding`,
401 on `api.moonshot.cn`) — usable only through coding-agent front-ends.

## Dead ends (do NOT retry)

- The abstract-adjoint **mate-δ route** via `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).Monoidal`
  / `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` (4 iters dead; needs general-`extendScalars`
  monoidality lift — strictly harder than H2's ring-iso case).
- "Sectionwise unfold `PresheafOfModules.pullback`" — it is an opaque left adjoint;
  no sectionwise formula; kaehler lane excised the analogue.
- Adding `IsLocallyTrivial M N` hypotheses — unnecessary (lemma is true for
  arbitrary `M N`) and there is no `SheafOfModules` iso-gluing primitive.

open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
input.new_string:
open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — base change along a ring iso commutes
with `⊗` (the H2 "bottom gap" of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`)

For a *ring isomorphism* `e : R ≃+* S` and `S`-modules `A`, `B`, base change along
`e` (giving each `S`-module its `R`-module structure via `Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom`)
commutes with the tensor product: the canonical map `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b` is an
`R`-linear equivalence `A ⊗[R] B ≃ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B`. Equivalently, `restrictScalars`
along a ring iso is *strong* monoidal — the lax tensorator is invertible. Mathlib
has `ModuleCat.extendScalars` strong monoidal but `restrictScalars` only
`LaxMonoidal`; this ring-iso strong upgrade is absent and is the documented "REAL
bottom gap" (H2) of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/

section RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor

open TensorProduct

variable {R S : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]

/-- The `R`-linear equivalence `A ⊗[R] B ≃ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B` (`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ b`),
where the `R`-module structures are base-changed along the ring iso `e : R ≃+* S`.
Base change along a ring iso commutes with `⊗`. -/
noncomputable def restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)
    (A B : Type u) [AddCommGroup A] [Module S A] [AddCommGroup B] [Module S B] :
    letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
    letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
    letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
    TensorProduct R A B ≃ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B := by
  letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
  letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
  letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
  -- `S`-module structure on the `R`-tensor, base-changed along `e.symm`, used to
  -- build the inverse out of the `S`-tensor.
  letI _iTR : Module S (TensorProduct R A B) := Module.compHom _ e.symm.toRingHom
  -- Forward: `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`.
  refine LinearEquiv.ofLinear
    (TensorProduct.lift
      { toFun := fun a =>
          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[S] b
            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
            map_smul' := fun r b => by
              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
              show a ⊗ₜ[S] (e r • b) = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
              rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul] }
        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul]
        map_smul' := fun r a => by
          ext b
          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk,
            AddHom.coe_mk]
          show (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[S] b = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.smul_tmul] })
    (TensorProduct.lift
      { toFun := fun a =>
          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[R] b
            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
            map_smul' := fun s b => by
              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
              show a ⊗ₜ[R] (e.symm s • b) = e.symm s • (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)
              rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul] }
        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul]
        map_smul' := fun s a => by
          ext b
          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk,
            AddHom.coe_mk]
          show (e.symm s • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = e.symm s • (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)
          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.smul_tmul] } : _ →ₗ[R] _)
    ?_ ?_
  · -- right inverse: on `a ⊗ₜ[S] b`
    ext a b
    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, TensorProduct.lift.tmul,
      LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
  · -- left inverse: on `a ⊗ₜ[R] b`
    ext a b
    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, TensorProduct.lift.tmul,
      LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]

end RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor

  -- Forward: `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`.
  refine LinearEquiv.ofLinear
    (TensorProduct.lift

            map_smul' := fun r b => by

-- `S`-module structure on the `R`-tensor, base-changed along `e.symm`, used to
  -- build the inverse out of the `S`-tensor.
  letI _iTR : Module S (TensorProduct R A B) := Module.compHom _ e.symm.toRingHom
  -- Forward: `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`, an `R`-bilinear-to-linear lift.
  let fwd : TensorProduct R A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B :=
    TensorProduct.lift
      { toFun := fun a =>
          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[S] b
            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
            map_smul' := fun r b => by
              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
              show a ⊗ₜ[S] (e r • b) = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
              rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul] }
        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul]
        map_smul' := fun r a => by
          ext b
          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk,
            AddHom.coe_mk]
          show (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[S] b = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.smul_tmul] }
  -- Inverse, first as an `S`-linear map out of the `S`-tensor into the
  -- `e.symm`-base-changed `R`-tensor, then reinterpreted as `R`-linear.
  let bwd₀ : TensorProduct S A B →ₗ[S] TensorProduct R A B :=
    TensorProduct.lift
      { toFun := fun a =>
          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[R] b
            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
            map_smul' := fun s b => by
              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
              show a ⊗ₜ[R] (s • b) = s • (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)
              -- target `s •` is the `e.symm`-base-changed action `_iTR`
              show a ⊗ₜ[R] (s • b) = e.symm s • (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)
              rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
              congr 1
              show s • b = (e.symm s : R) • b
              show s • b = (e (e.symm s)) • b
              rw [e.apply_symm_apply] }
        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul]
        map_smul' := fun s a => by
          ext b
          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk,
            AddHom.coe_mk]
          show (s • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = e.symm s • (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)
          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.smul_tmul]
          congr 1
          show s • a = (e (e.symm s)) • a
          rw [e.apply_symm_apply] }
  let bwd : TensorProduct S A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct R A B :=
    { toFun := bwd₀
      map_add' := bwd₀.map_add
      map_smul' := fun r x => by
        simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
        show bwd₀ (e r • x) = r • bwd₀ x
        rw [map_smul]
        show (e r : S) • bwd₀ x = r • bwd₀ x
        show e.symm (e r) • bwd₀ x = r • bwd₀ x
        rw [e.symm_apply_apply] }
  refine LinearEquiv.ofLinear fwd bwd ?_ ?_
  · -- right inverse `fwd ∘ bwd = id` on `a ⊗ₜ[S] b`
    ext a b
    show fwd (bwd₀ (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
    simp only [bwd₀, fwd, TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
  · -- left inverse `bwd ∘ fwd = id` on `a ⊗ₜ[R] b`
    ext a b
    show bwd₀ (fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
    simp only [bwd₀, fwd, TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]

The backward direction is fighting cross-ring typeclass resolution (`SMulCommClass`/`CompatibleSMul`). The clean fix: build the inverse via `TensorProduct.liftAddHom` (additive lift with an explicit scalar-swap compatibility), avoiding the transported `S`-module structure entirely. Let me also fix the inverse proofs to use `TensorProduct.ext'`.

  letI _iTR : Module S (TensorProduct R A B) := Module.compHom _ e.symm.toRingHom

  let fwd : TensorProduct R A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B :=
    TensorProduct.lift
      { toFun := fun a =>
          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[S] b
            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
            map_smul' := fun r b => by
              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
              show a ⊗ₜ[S] (e r • b) = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
              rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul] }
        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul]
        map_smul' := fun r a => by
          ext b
          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk,
            AddHom.coe_mk]
          show (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[S] b = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.smul_tmul] }
  -- Inverse, first as an `S`-linear map out of the `S`-tensor into the
  -- `e.symm`-base-changed `R`-tensor, then reinterpreted as `R`-linear.
  let bwd₀ : TensorProduct S A B →ₗ[S] TensorProduct R A B :=
    TensorProduct.lift
      { toFun := fun a =>
          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[R] b
            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
            map_smul' := fun s b => by
              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]

-- Forward: `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`, an `R`-bilinear-to-linear lift.
  let fwd : TensorProduct R A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B :=
    TensorProduct.lift
      { toFun := fun a =>
          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[S] b
            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
            map_smul' := fun r b => by
              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
              show a ⊗ₜ[S] (e r • b) = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
              rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul] }
        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul]
        map_smul' := fun r a => by
          ext b
          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk,
            AddHom.coe_mk]
          show (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[S] b = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.smul_tmul] }
  -- Inverse: `a ⊗ₜ[S] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[R] b`. Built as an additive lift out of the
  -- `S`-tensor (scalar-swap compatibility uses `s • a = e.symm s •ᵣ a`), then
  -- shown `R`-linear (`R` acting on the `S`-tensor via `e`).
  let bwdAdd : TensorProduct S A B →+ TensorProduct R A B :=
    TensorProduct.liftAddHom
      { toFun := fun a =>
          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[R] b
            map_zero' := by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add] }
        map_zero' := by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.zero_tmul]
        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
      (fun s a b => by
        simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
        -- `(s • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = a ⊗ₜ[R] (s • b)`; move the `S`-scalar through `e.symm`.
        have hsa : (s • a) = (e.symm s : R) • a := by
          show s • a = e (e.symm s) • a; rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
        have hsb : (s • b) = (e.symm s : R) • b := by
          show s • b = e (e.symm s) • b; rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
        rw [hsa, hsb, TensorProduct.smul_tmul'] )
  let bwd : TensorProduct S A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct R A B :=
    { toFun := bwdAdd
      map_add' := bwdAdd.map_add
      map_smul' := fun r x => by
        simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
        -- `R` acts on the `S`-tensor via `e`; reduce to additive `S`-scalar action.
        show bwdAdd (e r • x) = r • bwdAdd x
        induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
        | zero => simp
        | tmul a b =>
            rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
            show bwdAdd ((e r • a) ⊗ₜ[S] b) = r • bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
            show (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = r • (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)
            have : (e r • a) = (r : R) • a := by
              show e r • a = e r • a; rfl
            rw [show (e r • a : A) = (r : R) • a from by
              show e r • a = e r • a; rfl, TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
        | add x y hx hy =>
            rw [smul_add, map_add, map_add, hx, hy, smul_add] }
  refine LinearEquiv.ofLinear fwd bwd ?_ ?_
  · -- right inverse `fwd ∘ bwd = id` on `a ⊗ₜ[S] b`
    refine TensorProduct.ext' fun a b => ?_
    show fwd (bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
    show fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
    simp only [fwd, TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
  · -- left inverse `bwd ∘ fwd = id` on `a ⊗ₜ[R] b`
    refine TensorProduct.ext' fun a b => ?_
    show bwdAdd (fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
    show bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
    rfl

## What I built — `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv`

```lean
noncomputable def restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)
    (A B : Type u) [AddCommGroup A] [Module S A] [AddCommGroup B] [Module S B] :
    letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
    letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
    letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
    TensorProduct R A B ≃ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B
```

i.e. **base change of modules along a ring isomorphism commutes with `⊗`**: the
canonical map `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b` is an `R`-linear equivalence. Equivalently,
`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring iso is *strong* monoidal (its lax tensorator
is invertible) — the exact Mathlib-absent fact the H2 step of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`
bottoms out in (Mathlib has `extendScalars` strong monoidal but `restrictScalars`
only `LaxMonoidal`).

**Proof structure** (all in-file, axiom-clean):
- `fwd : A ⊗[R] B →ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B` via `TensorProduct.lift`; `R`-bilinearity of
  `mk_S` holds because the `R`-action is `r • x = e r • x` (`Module.compHom`),
  matched through `TensorProduct.tmul_smul` / `smul_tmul'`.
- `bwd : A ⊗[S] B →ₗ[R] A ⊗[R] B` via `TensorProduct.liftAddHom` (additive lift
  out of the `S`-tensor, scalar-swap compatibility `s • a = e.symm s •ᵣ a` from
  `e.apply_symm_apply`), then promoted to `R`-linear by `TensorProduct.induction_on`
  on the `R`-via-`e` action. **Key trick that unblocked the cross-ring friction**:
  building the inverse as an `AddHom` (not a transported `S`-linear map) sidesteps
  the `SMulCommClass`/`CompatibleSMul` synthesis failures that an `S`-bilinear lift
  into an `e.symm`-base-changed module triggers.
- Round-trips: left inverse via `TensorProduct.ext'` (over `R`); right inverse via
  `LinearMap.ext` + `TensorProduct.induction_on` (the composite is `R`-linear over
  the `S`-tensor, so `ext'` does not type — induction is required).

## Why the assigned gate target was not closed

The FINAL gate asked to close `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` (sorry count
must decrease) or escalate to USER in iter-216. After a real attempt I confirm the
**escalation branch is the correct outcome**: every route to that sorry bottoms out
in a confirmed multi-iter Mathlib-absent ingredient. Independently re-verified this
iter via `lean_leansearch`/`lean_loogle`:

1. **PRIMARY (locally-trivial) route is not statable at the current signature.**
   `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is over a *general site* `C` with an
   *arbitrary presheaf* `F : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)`. The PRIMARY
   proof in the blueprint is *sheaf-level* — it uses `tensorObj_restrict_iso` + the
   left unitor on a trivialising cover `F|_V ≅ 𝒪_V`, all `Scheme.Modules`-level.
   `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial` cannot even be *stated* for a general-site
   presheaf `F`. "Specialise to `[IsLocallyTrivial F]`" therefore means re-signing
   the lemma (and its consumers `W_whiskerLeft/Right_of_W` → the already-CLOSED
   `tensorObj_assoc_iso`) down to `Scheme.Modules` over `Opens X` — a large refactor
   that (a) risks breaking the currently-green assembled `tensorObj_assoc_iso`, and
   (b) **still needs `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, which is itself an open sorry**. So
   the PRIMARY route cannot decrease the count without first closing
   `tensorObj_restrict_iso`.
2. **FALLBACK (stalkwise) route needs d.2.** Confirmed Mathlib-absent: the stalk-⊗
   commutation over a *varying* ring (`(A ⊗ᵖ B)_x ≅ A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`). The
   analogist (`ts-d2-215`) already rated this ~150–250 LOC multi-iter. The d.1-core
   (`stalkLinearMap*`) is built but insufficient alone.
3. **`tensorObj_restrict_iso` (the linchpin both the PRIMARY route and
   `exists_tensorObj_inverse` depend on) is ~200–300 LOC across 4 absent
   ingredients** (`informal/tensorObj_restrict_iso.md`). I attacked its single most
   self-contained, reusable piece — the H2 bottom gap — and **closed it**
   (`restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv`). The remaining gaps are genuinely larger:
   - **H1 (linchpin, still absent):** presheaf-level `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`
     via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`, needing a presheaf-level
     `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (only the SHEAF version exists; confirmed absent via
     loogle) plus presheaf-level `pushforwardNatTrans`/`pushforwardCongr`. ~100–150 LOC.
   - **H2 remainder (now small):** package the new equiv as
     `IsIso (μ (restrictScalars (α.app X)))` (identify `μ`'s underlying map with the
     equiv via `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul`, then `LinearEquiv ⇒ IsIso`), lift
     to the presheaf `μ` (`toPresheaf` reflects isos), feed
     `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal` (`IsIso ε` is easy — `ε` is the ring iso).

So: a genuine attempt was made on the linchpin; one of its four ingredients is now
closed in-file; the remaining residual is precisely H1 + the small H2 packaging.

sub-lemma under `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal` (the strong upgrade of the existing
lax instance). No `\leanok` action by me (provers don't touch markers).

## Concrete next step (iter-216, if substrate continued rather than escalated)

1. Add `restrictScalars_μ_isIso (e : R ≃+* S) (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat S) :
   IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂)` —
   thin wrapper: `μ`'s underlying map `= restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` by
   `restrictScalars_μ_tmul` + `TensorProduct.ext`, hence `IsIso` (~20 LOC).
2. Upgrade the file's `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal` to `Monoidal` via
   `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal` (needs `IsIso ε` + the presheaf `IsIso μ` lifted
   from (1) by `toPresheaf` reflecting isos).
3. Build H1 (presheaf-level `pushforwardPushforwardAdj`) — the genuine remaining
   `mathlib-build`-scale obstacle. Then `tensorObj_restrict_iso` closes, which in
   turn unblocks the PRIMARY route for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` and

## Dead ends (do NOT retry)

- Building the inverse `bwd` as an `S`-linear `TensorProduct.lift` into an
  `e.symm`-base-changed `R`-tensor: fails with `SMulCommClass R S A` /

  manual `R`-linearity instead (what the committed proof does).
- `TensorProduct.ext'` for the right-inverse: the composite `fwd ∘ bwd` is
  `R`-linear over the `S`-tensor, so `ext'` (which expects `S`-linear) type-mismatches;
  use `LinearMap.ext` + `TensorProduct.induction_on`.

## Why I stopped — verdict: **Partial progress**

Closed the H2 "REAL bottom gap" of `tensorObj_restrict_iso` as a new, fully-proved,
axiom-clean `LinearEquiv` (`restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv`, ~75 LOC) — genuine
in-file code, not prose. Did **not** close any of the 4 top-level sorries: each
